title: lichen_l2.c:1550 double-zeroing peer_table on re-init path
description:
In lichen_l2_iface_init(), when link_ctx_initialized is true (re-initialization path):
1. Line 1550: secure_zero(peer_table, sizeof(peer_table)) is called under mutexes
2. Line 1578: secure_zero(peer_table, sizeof(peer_table)) is called again unconditionally

The second secure_zero at line 1578 is redundant on the re-init path. The comment at lines 1571-1577 acknowledges this: 'On re-init, peer_table was already cleared with secure_zero above while holding mutexes. This secure_zero is redundant in that case but harmless.'

SEVERITY: P4 - minor inefficiency, no correctness bug. The code is correct, just slightly wasteful of CPU cycles on the re-init path.

NO ACTION NEEDED - documented and intentional for code clarity.
